sections.heading: Primary Treatment of Early-Stage Vulvar SCC/Adenocarcinoma (Stage I and Select Stage II)
sections.content:
Criteria: Stage IA (≤1 mm invasion), lateral lesion (≥2 cm from midline), clinically negative nodes. Options: Surgery - Simple partial vulvectomy (preferred). Recommendation: Observe after resection if negative margins; if pathology reveals >1 mm invasion, additional surgery may be warranted. Adjuvant: No adjuvant therapy needed for Stage IA with negative margins.

Criteria: Stage IB (>1 mm invasion) or select Stage II, lateral lesion (≥2 cm from midline), clinically negative nodes. Options: Surgery - Radical partial vulvectomy + ipsilateral inguinofemoral lymph node evaluation (SLNB or lymphadenectomy) (preferred). Recommendation: Sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNB) or ipsilateral inguinofemoral lymphadenectomy; if SLNB not detected, complete lymphadenectomy required. Adjuvant: Therapy based on primary tumor risk factors (close margins, LVSI, tumor size, depth of invasion, pattern of invasion) and nodal pathology. If negative margins and nodes, observe; if positive margins, re-excision or adjuvant EBRT; if nodal metastases, adjuvant RT ± chemotherapy.

Criteria: Stage IB or select Stage II, near midline or midline lesions (anterior or posterior), clinically negative nodes. Options: Surgery - Radical partial vulvectomy + bilateral inguinofemoral lymph node evaluation (SLNB or lymphadenectomy) (preferred). Recommendation: Bilateral SLNB or bilateral inguinofemoral lymphadenectomy; if SLNB not detected on side, complete lymphadenectomy required. Adjuvant: Therapy based on primary tumor risk factors and nodal status as above.

sections.heading: Adjuvant Therapy Based on Primary Tumor Risk Factors
sections.content:
Criteria: Negative margins after resection. Options: Observation - Surveillance (preferred); Radiation Therapy - Adjuvant EBRT to primary site (other recommended). Recommendation: Observe if no other high-risk features; consider EBRT for other invasive disease risk factors (close margins, LVSI, tumor size, depth of invasion, pattern of invasion). Adjuvant: No adjuvant therapy for negative margins without high-risk factors.

Criteria: Positive margins for invasive disease. Options: Surgery - Re-excision (preferred); Radiation Therapy - Adjuvant EBRT to primary site (alternative). Recommendation: Re-excision to achieve negative margins; if not feasible or margins remain positive, use EBRT. Adjuvant: Re-excision or adjuvant EBRT for positive margins.

sections.heading: Adjuvant Therapy Based on Nodal Evaluation
sections.content:
Criteria: Lymph nodes negative (sentinel or inguinofemoral). Options: Observation - Surveillance (preferred). Recommendation: No adjuvant nodal therapy. Adjuvant: No adjuvant nodal therapy for negative nodes.

Criteria: Single positive SLN ≤2 mm metastasis. Options: Radiation Therapy - EBRT ± concurrent chemotherapy (preferred). Recommendation: Adjuvant RT to groin ± chemotherapy; category 2A. Adjuvant: Adjuvant RT ± chemotherapy for micrometastatic nodal disease.

Criteria: Positive SLN >2 mm metastasis. Options: Surgery - Complete inguinofemoral lymphadenectomy (preferred); Radiation Therapy - EBRT ± concurrent chemotherapy (alternative). Recommendation: Lymphadenectomy followed by EBRT ± concurrent chemotherapy; if not suitable for surgery, use EBRT ± chemo. Adjuvant: Adjuvant RT ± chemotherapy for macrometastatic nodal disease.
